message:
I remember the winter storm that caught everyone off guard last year. My neighbors were scrambling for flashlights and extra blankets, while I was feeling a strange sense of calm. You see, for once, I had done my homework. I had stocked up on food, water, and even had a cozy supply of candles ready to go. It felt good to be the one sharing tips instead of the one panicking!

That evening, as the snow piled up outside, I invited a few neighbors over for hot cocoa, and we all huddled together, sharing stories and laughs. It turned into a cozy little gathering instead of a crisis. 

Being prepared doesn’t just mean having supplies; it also means being the calm in the storm for yourself and others. If you’re ever in doubt about what to pack, this little refresher helped me a lot! What’s your favorite cozy activity to do when the weather keeps you inside?
